title: exocarpos latifolius
intro: Exocarpos latifolius, also known as the Woolly-bur or Strawberry-burr, is a small shrub native to Australia and Southeast Asia, characterized by its unique, woolly foliage and small white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. The health benefits of Exocarpos latifolius include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
